experiences.intentions: Two boxes: (2,1) is directly below target (1,1), and pushing it up would solve one target immediately. Check deadlocks: (2,1) is against left wall but target is along that wall at (1,1), so this is safe. Player at (1,2) can move left onto the target square (1,1), then from there be above the box to push it up. Best first action is reposition left.
experiences.tasks: Push all boxes onto goal positions in the Sokoban puzzle.

experiences.intentions: Two boxes: (2,1) is directly below target (1,1), so the best first goal is to push that box up onto the target. To do that, the player must stand at (3,1), below the box. Since the game just started and the recent move was left, the player is likely near the central lower area; the immediate safe repositioning step is to move down toward the lower corridor to approach beneath the left box. This avoids pushing any box into a wall or corner and sets up the simple solve of the left target first.
experiences.tasks: Push all boxes onto goal positions in the Sokoban puzzle.

experiences.intentions: We need act purposefully. Infer player position from recent moves: likely started around (5,3); after left, down, left the player is plausibly at (6?) but border blocks, so more likely actual current position is (5,1) or nearby. Board display omits player, but reflection says reposition only. Strategic target: box at (2,1) can go onto target (1,1) by pushing up, but player must stand at (3,1). If player is currently at (5,1), the best next step is up toward that position. This avoids deadlocks and sets up solving first box.

Check accessibility: (3,1) is floor, and the player is likely near there after recent moves. Also, pushing the other box first is less direct. Best immediate action is to move or push toward solving box (2,1). Since box (2,1) can be pushed up safely onto the target and this does not create deadlock, choose that push if adjacent below the box.
